evals.name: missing-dependency-stops
evals.prompt: Run agent-self-harness on a provided trace packet, but agent-weakness-miner is not installed. What should happen?
evals.expected_output: The workflow stops before trace discovery or analysis and prints setup instructions for installing agent-weakness-miner from the same source package as agent-self-harness.
expectations: The output names the missing dependency
The output says to stop before normalization or raw trace inspection
The output gives setup instructions
The output does not scaffold a replacement skill

evals.name: source-selection-routes-codex-claude-explicit
evals.prompt: Use agent-self-harness to inspect execution history. The user has not said whether to use Codex sessions, Claude Code sessions, or an explicit packet file.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks for source and discovery mode, describing Codex local sessions, Claude Code local sessions, and explicit normalized packets as options.
expectations: The output asks for source selection before inspecting history
Codex local session discovery is listed
Claude Code local session discovery is listed
Explicit normalized packets are listed

evals.name: gate-one-blocks-candidate-work
evals.prompt: agent-weakness-miner returned three weakness packets. Continue the self-harness run.
evals.expected_output: The skill presents compact weakness packets and stops for the user to choose exactly one weakness before candidates, worktrees, eval edits, or file edits.
expectations: The output presents weakness choices compactly
The output explicitly requests user selection
The output states no candidate work starts before selection
No candidate worktree or edit is proposed as already applied

evals.name: candidate-ranking-quality-before-efficiency
evals.prompt: Two candidates passed targeted evals. Candidate A scores 92% with 120k tokens. Candidate B scores 86% with 40k tokens. Which wins?
evals.expected_output: Candidate A wins because behavior quality is not materially similar; token savings do not override a meaningful quality difference.
expectations: The output chooses the higher-quality candidate
The output explains that efficiency is a tie-breaker only after material similarity
The output mentions token usage as secondary

evals.name: no-safe-improvement
evals.prompt: All candidate changes either failed targeted evals or caused regression. Prepare the recommendation.
evals.expected_output: The skill reports no safe improvement found and does not recommend applying any candidate.
expectations: The output says no safe improvement was found
The output summarizes failed candidates or regressions
The output does not force a winner

evals.name: final-brief-decision-grade
evals.prompt: A best candidate was selected and it adds a helper script. Prepare Gate 2.
evals.expected_output: The final approval brief includes what improved, why the candidate won, eval results, token usage, runtime, risks, rollback, new script details, and an ASCII diagram if workflow or tooling changed.
expectations: The brief asks for explicit approval before apply
The brief includes eval results and why the candidate won
The brief includes token usage and runtime
The brief documents the new script purpose, inputs, outputs, location, network or secrets needs
The brief includes rollback notes
